title: Entity-Level Declarative Constraints
text: Entity-Level Declarative Constraints The following declarative constraints exist at entity level: If compound primary or candidate keys are defined, all fields in the key must contain data (by default, incomplete keys fail validation). The total number of occurrences in the entity must be between the minimum and maximum specified in the entity properties. By default, this constraint is not defined. Compound Keys Must Be Complete When a compound primary or candidate key is validated, Uniface first checks if the key is complete. A complete key means that all fields belonging to that key contain data, except for fields that have a syntax definition of LEN(0-0). If the key is not complete, Uniface sets $error to 2206 and activates the entity-level error trigger. The validation process fails, and the validateKey trigger (or any other trigger) is not activated. Minimum and Maximum Number of Occurrences The minimum and maximum number of occurrences can be specified when defining modeled entities and component entities in the Uniface IDE. When an entity is validated, Uniface checks whether the number of occurrences is within the range of the minimum and maximum specified. If this validation check fails, Uniface sets $error to 0102 or 0103, depending on the violated definition, and activates the entity-level error trigger. The function $error is set to: 0102 if the number of occurrences is less than the minimum 0103 if the number of occurrences is greater than the maximum Note: Occurrences created with ProcScript are validated before declarative validation for the entity is performed.
